workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Just got this from Michael Petrov at Humongous Insurance -- I need help figuring out account health. Manager is asking for an update today.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Humongous Insurance
Date: 2024-02-12
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into account health.

[00:06:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:08:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:10:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:14:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-N trap (Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output ); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-N failure mode (Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output )
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-N -- Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
